I’m thrilled to introduce an extraordinary opportunity for you...

This sponsored message from Freedom Nation Today is on behalf of Hillsdale College

| | | [](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=15c254ca0931454d92fd7307f77ff5f7&_e=W_fd1eMbg8bo5V_59NehCqxjmjdzcKDwBROZrVUW67xNWN-WTM5Xei5wildZCxsq39UjDfelisbgTeWzDo5XwGzRfDOnV9jMuEK-fKRGHVPZ9k6ZYErwfPUNvBjJZopHx7mczbtch1tpWXiJr9tqSmYUyABdtw4AXBCato58qfpE1Bnu8TPORt_cYOsJo2lYyLJfD_3947uNoyp_D9Yn6NkhPjVrerUjW5obYbReXfb3EdrNBLD1_jykpQclYvZRe-lSIRq7CgFhLxByDLqXy3D4qnA7x3SQYfAwRkAuv_u9DZ8l4_v7CFRxB8ZNUaaJDFXlbIA1t2x4lHjNF18fwI2xNOrWuDN3ETM2JQgfJiAWbcS0UXqM5CZcEgK4CQFwE7LdVHcIi8AgxDBkXN9ODYlgryWPjqFnhpuHN7B_C_4%3D)

| | Dear Friend,

I'm thrilled to introduce an extraordinary opportunity for you...

Perhaps you've wondered where the artwork accompanying the lectures in our free online courses comes from. We hear this especially for one of our most popular courses, "The Genesis Story."

The Book of Genesis has inspired some of Western Civilization's greatest artists to produce iconic works. You may be familiar with Michelangelo's "The Creation of Adam" and Rembrandt's "The Sacrifice of Issac."

I can still remember seeing "The Creation of Adam" right after I graduated from Hillsdale at the Sistine Chapel...

[](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=15c254ca0931454d92fd7307f77ff5f7&_e=W_fd1eMbg8bo5V_59NehCqxjmjdzcKDwBROZrVUW67xNWN-WTM5Xei5wildZCxsq39UjDfelisbgTeWzDo5XwGzRfDOnV9jMuEK-fKRGHVPZ9k6ZYErwfPUNvBjJZopHx7mczbtch1tpWXiJr9tqSmYUyABdtw4AXBCato58qfpE1Bnu8TPORt_cYOsJo2lYyLJfD_3947uNoyp_D9Yn6NkhPjVrerUjW5obYbReXfb3EdrNBLD1_jykpQclYvZRe-lSIRq7CgFhLxByDLqXy3N9dN_1lihREze174nYvJ3TB7Zz3vOb9p7f3QkjxZG9o2PT6yF7hsaywASRAKOh_G6gBGfOhAoaF-HxvP8Ii96gDEr_JfNPx5g3NtZBQ0jE7HxhsxpjxP4b7RznaHOCEHrvD1xmgG0GQA7DlzNibYI%3D) **We 've compiled these paintings, along with many others featured in this course, into a brand-new coffee table book, "The Genesis Story: Course Companion." And I'd like you to receive a copy.**

With this exclusive book--available only from Hillsdale College--you can read the passages from the Book of Genesis that led Michelangelo, Rembrandt, Gustave Dore, and other masters to create these incredible works of art.

And with these works all in one place, you and your family can enjoy page after page of divinely inspired art.

**You can reserve your copy of "The Genesis Story: Course Companion," an exclusive hard-cover book from Hillsdale College, for a generous donation of $100 or more by clicking the secure link below: **

**[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/genesis-story-course-companion-book](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=15c254ca0931454d92fd7307f77ff5f7&_e=W_fd1eMbg8bo5V_59NehCqxjmjdzcKDwBROZrVUW67xNWN-WTM5Xei5wildZCxsq39UjDfelisbgTeWzDo5XwGzRfDOnV9jMuEK-fKRGHVPZ9k6ZYErwfPUNvBjJZopHx7mczbtch1tpWXiJr9tqSmYUyABdtw4AXBCato58qfpE1Bnu8TPORt_cYOsJo2lYyLJfD_3947uNoyp_D9Yn6NkhPjVrerUjW5obYbReXfb3EdrNBLD1_jykpQclYvZRe-lSIRq7CgFhLxByDLqXy1ELv4tp9gsikySBYLKoJr99tW4UnmmSbsdmIovLJwXFxoyvFtnD2cZIS3Ixh6wXp0i0IHY1IdYqJ2iRADC44d0UClfJszB7pqMrKJjWGqpiX1HsccSUJLjeZ_UHTFbAUNV3ErGA7CmtG2YmYe22viY%3D)**

Thanks again for your continued support and generosity. I hope you'll enjoy this beautiful book.

Warm regards,

Bill Gray, '01
Vice President
Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College

And with these works all in one place, you and your family can enjoy page after page of divinely inspired art. You can reserve your copy of "The Genesis Story: Course Companion," an exclusive hard-cover book from Hillsdale College, for a generous donation of $100 or more by clicking the secure link below: Thanks again for your continued support and generosity. I hope you'll enjoy this beautiful book. Warm regards, Bill Gray, '01 Vice President Institutional Advancement Hillsdale College | | Freedom Nation Today PO Box 2221 Fort Collins, CO 80522 | _

name: Amanda Chase
subject: Rhetoric versus Record

| | |
---
|
# Who has a PROVEN TRACK RECORD?
#

Who has the COURAGE TO STAND UP FOR YOU?
---
|
---
|
# AMANDA CHASE
---
| Friends, Many of you called me "Trump in Heels." I was honored. Like President Trump I've been fighting the Swamp in Richmond, since 2008 when I first got involved in Virginia politics as a young mother of 4, 3 of those were sons by the way.
Many of you have said you are supporting me because I have a backbone and I'm not afraid to stand up for you. I was censured over January 6 and saying the 2020 Presidential Election was stolen. They built a plexiglass wall around my desk for refusing to wear a mask.
I learned from my father, who retired as an engineer and my mother who was a school teacher, to ask questions and to think critically.
I've served in the Virginia Senate for 8 years from 2016-2023 before retiring to Appomattox, Virginia.
Now I live in Senate District 10 and I am seeking the Republican nomination for Virginia Senate in a Special Election this coming December to finish off John McGuire's term in the Virginia Senate, as he goes off to Congress this January.
I have both the experience and conservative voting record you can trust to hit the ground running on day one.
If you live in Senate District 10 which includes 11 counties, Amelia, Appomattox, Buckingham, Cumberland, Prince Edward, Fluvanna, Goochland, Hanover, the Shady Grove precinct in Henrico, Louisa and Powhatan, you will have the opportunity to vote in a Special Election in a matter of weeks, to fill a state senate seat that John McGuire will vacate as he heads to Congress.
This Special Election will most likely take place mid-December in a Republican run off called a Republican mass meeting. Once the exact date and location are decided we will let you know.
While there are several candidates vying for the Republican nomination, I'm the only candidate who has actually served in the Virginia Senate as a Senator for 8 years and has an actual conservative voting record you can trust.
Any candidate can say they are a conservative or they are pro-2A, but I'm the only candidate running with an actual 8 year voting record you can trust, not just rhetoric.
When the going gets tough, will your representative cave to the pressure to "go along, to get along?" Will they be brave enough to ask the tough questions, the questions that you would ask?
Will your representative take a stand for you? Will they challenge the status quo, as I did during the assault on our liberties "during Covid," and after the 2020 Presidential Election was stolen?
Does the candidate you are supporting have a history of standing up to protect the unborn, medical freedom, the 2nd Amendment, true election integrity including hand counting paper ballots?
Talk is cheap. Do they have a history of standing up for what's right?
Where were they during the Covid mandates? Did they go along with the vaccine and mask mandates or did they rightly push back?
Where were they on January 6?
Greetings! I've been with you in the trenches fighting against the gun grab, the Covid mandates, the stolen elections and the fight against medical tyranny, just for starters.
People were dying from the jabs. People were getting injured from the jabs. Yet they were being mandated if they wanted to work, continue their education or even access certain venues. It should have been your choice, not a mandate.
Where were they?
I had to speak up. It was the right thing to do.
Our Constitutional rights were being decimated during Covid, and I refused to be silent.
Businesses were being crushed as government dictated which businesses were essential and which ones were not essential. So I organized a "Reopen Virginia" rally in Richmond which took place during Governor Northam's press conference. You could actually hear car horns beeping during his press conference.
Students were not allowed to return to college unless they were vaxxed. So I introduced legislation to protect students who did not want to get the experimental jab.
It seemed I was the only elected official talking about what was important to the people, unconstitutional Covid mandates and the 2020 stolen presidential election. The people knew they could trust me.
Voting and participation in our elections is vital to our Constitutional Republic. Yet there are some who want only the party faithful to be able to choose our Republican nominees and allow the masses to have a say in the General Election.
Do you remember the Governor's race?
You wanted to vote but the process was confusing. Where do you vote? When did you vote? Most had no idea when or where to vote and when they did find out, realized it was too late to sign up.
Most voters had no idea there was paperwork to be completed and deadlines in which they had to turn in required GOP created forms. It was the individual county or city GOP chairs that arbitrarily determined deadlines and locations for turning in those forms. Some unit chairs allowed several weeks to turn in the forms, other chairs only gave people a week.
Republican voters who wanted to participate in the nomination process for choosing our next Republican nominee for Governor found the voting process confusing. Many missed the arbitrary deadlines that were different for each county and many voters who wanted to participate could not. They had missed the arbitrary deadlines.
There were Republican voters who were not members of their local GOP who had a difficult time figuring out how to vote.
In a Congressional run off, my elderly parents had to wait hours in 100 degree heat during the height of the Covid mandates.
What are we doing? We are discouraging people from participating in the process.
When I returned the Senate, I voted to end confusing party processes that disenfranchise and discourage Republicans from voting and choosing our Republican nominees. And I was successful.
While some Republicans support this confusing party processes to chose our Republican nominees, I consistently support processes that voters are most familiar with and allow legal recourse for bad actors.
This legislation went into effect July 2024 but does not apply to Special Elections, like the one we will have this coming December and January.
So please pay attention, so you don't miss the upcoming deadlines and can participate in the process to choose the next Republican nominee to replace John McGuire in the Virginia Senate.
If you live in Senate District 10, which includes Amelia, Appomattox, Buckingham, Cumberland, Prince Edward, Fluvanna, Goochland, Henrico, Hanover, Louisa or Powhatan, you will most likely vote in November, December and January. Please be on the look out after this November's General Election for a Special Election to be called to fill an open state senate seat to be vacated by John McGuire who who is expected to win this November.
Republicans will chose their nominee in December or January with the General Election expected to be January 7, the day before the General Assembly gavels in. So please stay tuned. There will be an announcement of a Special Election and then 5 days later we are expected to come up with a Republican nominee.
Typically a replacement would be selected by only a handful of party faithful who are aware of the election.
I started back in July educating you about this Special Election, so that you could be made aware and participate in selecting our Republican nominee.
It's important that McGuire's successor is chosen by "We the People". This is no time for inexperience, which is why I threw my hat in the ring to run back in July, as the first candidate to announce.
In July, I fully committed to running for this state senate seat and bought a home in conservative Appomattox, where I plan to reside indefinitely.
Those who have followed me know I have both the experience and conservative voting record to hit the ground running on day one, when session will already be in progress.
While there are many Republicans vying for the seat, I'm the only candidate who will be ready day one when session is already in progress.
Talk is cheap. Actions count.
While many will say they support issues important to conservatives, yet I'm the only one running with an actual conservative voting record to prove it.
While there are a number of candidates seeking the Republican nomination for State Senate in Senate District 10 to finish John McGuire's state senate term, I'm the only candidate who has a proven voting record you can trust 100% of the time.
I have a conservative voting record you can trust all 8 years I served in the Virginia Senate:
100% Pro-family, The Family Foundations scorecard, 2016-2023
100% Pro-gun, VCDL Virginia Citizen's Defense League, 2016-2023
A rating, NRA, 2016-2023
100% Pro-small business, National Federation of Independent Business, 2016-2023.
You can trust me. I'm experienced. I have a conservative voting record you can trust, and I'm willing to stand when it's important to stand on our conservative principles. Sometimes that means speaking up and challenging the status quo.
You have the opportunity to support an award winning effective conservative senator with 8 years of experience as a senator and a 100 percent conservative voting record you can trust and who knows how to win for the people.
Today, I ask for your vote if you live in Amelia, Appomattox, Buckingham, Cumberland, Fluvanna, Goochland, Louisa, Prince Edward, Henrico, Hanover and Powhatan in the upcoming Special Election.
If you live outside the district, I ask for your most generous donation. You may not be able to vote for me, but I can certainly vote for you.
---

# KEY LEGISLATIVE WINS
---
|
* Healthcare Transparency Act
* Parents & Educators Not Big Business Determine School Start Times
* Elimination of Mask Mandates in Public Schools
* Historic Coal Ash Legislation to Reduce Cancer Risks
* Military Retirement Benefit Exemption
* Required Notification to Parents of Sexually Explicit Material in Public Schools
* Committee Meeting Online, Citizens Access to Legislators Online

* Doesn't Take PAC or Lobbyists Money
* Voted no on all fees and taxes
* Voted against all gun control measures
* 100% Pro-Life from fertilization

---
|
# LEGISLATIVE AWARDS & ACHIEVEMENTS
---
|
* 100% VCDL Virginia Citizen's Defense League, 8 years
* 100% The Family Foundation, 8 years
* 100% National Federation of Independent Business, 8 years
* "A" Rating NRA National Rifle Association
* "Conservative Excellence", The American Conservative Union, 8 years
* Legislator of the Year, Treasurer's Association of Virginia
* "A" Rating, Distinguished Advocate for Business, The Virginia Chamber of Commerce
* Patriot's Award, VAIB Virginia Assembly of Independent Baptists
* Outstanding Leadership, Sierra Club, In recognition of Leadership on Coal Ash
* James River Association River Hero, James River Association
* Industrial Strength Leadership Award, VMA Virginia Manufacturer's Association PAC

* Veterans For America First, 2023
* Virginia Society for Human Life, 2019

---
|
# BILLS I'VE INTRODUCED
---
| These are all of the bills that I've sponsored since I was first elected in 2016. These are Chase sponsored bills that I introduced and presented before the Virginia Senate from 2016 to 2023. There's no question where I stand on the issues. I'd like to point out a few highlights about the legislation I've introduced:

* Introduce term limit legislation EVERY year
* Paper ballots, hand counts, photo ID, day not a season, abolish drop boxes and no excuse absentee voting
* Abolish fees on exercising your right to bare arms
* Campaign finance reform to keep out of state dollars from influencing our elections
* Eliminate permanent absentee voting in which a live ballot is mailed out for every election
* Lowering taxes & red tape for businesses
* Reducing risk of cancer by proper storing of coal ash
* Eliminating government bureaucracies and increasing accountability and transparency
* More transparency in government
* Ensuring the first day of school is determined by parents and educators, not big business
* The ability to purchase health insurance across state lines
* Requirement to provide estimated cost of health care provider services prior to services being rendered
* More transparency in elections
* Reversal of all gun control legislation
* Voter Confidence Restoration Act to put the guardrails back on our elections
* Pro-life legislation
* Allowing school security officers to carry a firearm
* Protection from mask and vaccine mandates during Covid
* Bill of Rights for law enforcement
* No income tax for retirement benefits of our military
* Full forensic audit of our elections
* Charter School legislation
* Access to hydroxychloroquine and ivermectin without penalty
* No mutualization of our youth, SAFE Act
* Parental Choice Education Savings Accounts
* Parental Rights to Educational Transparency
* Parental advisory label to be affixed to sexually explicit materials in public libraries
* Property Owner consent to Smart Meters

|
# MY PERSONAL BIO
---
|
---
|
* Born in Sheffield, Alabama
* Currently live in Appomattox, Virginia
* Grew up in Bon Air, Virginia
* Lived in Virginia from 1979 to present
* Mother of 4 adult children
* Graduated from Virginia Tech
* Degree in Business, double majored in Finance & Management
* Worked in the finance, banking, credit industry in management
* Owned grassroots campaign management consulting business
* Homeschooled our 4 children

---
|
# MY FAITH IN JESUS
---
|
# WHY I STAND
---
| My faith in God through Jesus is central to who I am. It doesn't mean I'm perfect, but through all of the trials, persecution and struggles, it's my faith that I turn to and from which I draw strength during the good times and the more challenging times.
I accepted Jesus into my heart when I was in the 2nd grade. As a young teen and young adult my relationship with the Lord became more personal. I felt compelled to go on several missions trips and while in college at Virginia Tech was very involved with Campus Crusade for Christ, which is now called Cru.
It was during my college years that I began to study the Bible and my faith strengthened. I remember spending my spring break in Daytona sharing the gospel of Christ with people on the beach.
As a young adult who worked full time in management in the finance and banking field, I spent some week nights and weekends visiting people who had visited our church and led several small group Bible studies.
As a young mother, I left corporate America to raise and homeschool our 4 children under a religious exemption statue. It was then I began to volunteer for the Family Foundation and led various programs and coops for homeschool families seeking opportunities for their children. It was also then that I began to volunteer for various political campaigns for conservative Republicans.
One thing lead to another and my volunteerism lead to serving as campaign staff, political director and even campaign manager for various federal, state and local elected officials.
Ultimately, I would run and win a state senate seat, overcoming a 28 year incumbent. I served in the Virginia Senate for 8 years from 2016 to 2023, until I was "defeated" by 375 mail-in and early votes out of 22,000 votes, in which my campaign was denied overseeing of the early voting machines.
I contested the race, but was ultimately ignored by the Virginia State Board of Elections and the Republican Party of Virginia. We sought justice legally, but we were ignored. The people of Senate District 12 were disenfranchised but no one had the boldness to investigate our concern, despite our best efforts to fight the injustice. It was a frustrating experience but at the end of the day I trusted the Lord to ultimately bring justice.
Many of you have heard me say the Holy Spirit is my GPS. That is true.
Like many of you, I too get distracted and take the longer route because I wasn't paying attention. However, my heart is to put God first every morning, listen to His voice and follow the path that He has laid before me through doors that I believe He opens and closes. While I believe my loss was nefarious at best, it was a door God closed. And one I had a hard time accepting because of the appearance of cheating.
I've had former employees who had worked for me in the past and been good to literally stab me in the back. They accepted money from other elected officials who I had challenged on various policies to betray and defame my good character and reputation. They came after me politically, professionally and personally. A Richmond General District Court judge granted me a protective order against one employee who continues to follow me to this day.
Why? Because I wasn't popular with the other politicians. I wouldn't just go along to get along. I took a stand.
Life begins at conception, not 15 weeks, for starters. I refused to approve a Health and Human Resources Cabinet member because they refused to reverse Governor Northam's position regarding healthcare providers ability to prescribe ivermectin and hydroxychloroquine for the treatment of Covid.
I refused to rejoin the Republican Senate Caucus until the leader resigned because he had introduced bills to increase taxes and even introduced a gun control bill. I could not in good conscience sit under a leader who was introducing bills only a Democrat would introduce. Standing came at a cost.
I had my committee assignments taken away, my seniority taken away and my seat moved to the farthest side of the Virginia Senate chamber and my colleagues . All in an attempt to shame me into submission.
No matter what they did to me or said about me, I chose to stand.
---
|
# TAKING A STAND DURING COVID
---
|
# REFUSING TO WEAR A MASK
---
| During Covid, I refused to wear a mask. As a result, the Senate staff built a plexiglass wall around my desk. I referred to as my "Wall of Freedom."
Overnight, we saw America become a socialist nation. Our basic ability to breathe was now being governed by an overreaching tyrannical government.
Even the manufacturer of the masks printed a disclaimer stating the masks did not protect people from the spread of viruses. In fact, it was later proven that masks actually cause harm and increase a person's chances of contracting a respiratory virus.
I introduced many bills to make it illegal for employers, educators and even small businesses from violating our civil rights. These decisions of body autonomy we could determine for ourselves. I called on the government to stop restricting our ability to work, get educated or simply perform everyday activities like shop and visit the doctor for simply refusing the experimental jab.
I introduced legislation that would allow doctors to prescribe ivermectin and hydroxychloroquine for the treatment of Covid.
---
|
---
| Our Constitutional rights were being violated and I had to take a stand, no matter the cost. Wearing a mask or not should have been a choice, not a mandate.
---
|
---
| My colleagues complied with the mandates. I did not. I was both isolated and ostracized by my colleagues for standing. The Senate Clerk staff put my desk at the back of the room in a far corner. They tried to break me, but I stood strong.
---
|
---
| During the height of Covid, I was running for Governor. During the day I would serve in the Senate of Virginia. In the evenings, I campaigned across the state. No mask. No vaccine. People were desperate for someone who would stand up for them under the Constitutional mandates.
---
|
---
| Here's a picture of the Virginia Senate in 2021, during the height of Covid. We were forced to meet at the Science Museum of Virginia so that we could comply with social distancing mandates, which we've now discovered were a hoax and nothing more than political science.
---
|
---
| During the height of Covid, who stood up for you? Who fought the unconstitutional mandates? I did. And it came at a cost.
---
|
---
| Who can you count on to stand with you in the future? Who has had the courage, fortitude and history of standing with you in the past?
Standing wasn't easy. Even comedian Jimmy Kimmel mocked me nationally for refusing to wear a mask. They wanted to break me, but I stood strong in the midst of isolation, being mocked and ridiculed.
I continued to challenge the mask and vaccine mandates. They were making decisions based on political science, not science. Today, 4 years later, we know know the truth.
You can count on me. I've stood with you in the past. I will stand with you in the future.
---
| | [WRIC INTERVIEW](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001VMDFRILPhSHk0fhI9kIDrdsw_TeQ7pPtc8VL2G288kEk2GcMQXjdessr_DkEcgTl-G_26VlTrwsTtco_JHcIUvRSX7wNEfVx9AoB2UINZMqPQ3alilt6h1QadjtM_7QXuw1LsQjUII22RO-zm5P-zgR22j4w1Sdxmno7htA3RzFGjeHPkpP-ZX3NqLcsDwHKYNeKr5-IotLaL-CJMfSU8BnI0c5NWxRfP2zG8_P6Yr35vPPi2j17g2C9IPGNeNQU&c=aczNCFTWYdcYg8Ica-6UONvwf62rlw8GYd6FC84EKjixHCpHiJxidg==&ch=9igGIv7zSA2BLqbaZsJRG6Ro6CLZmrDfLbYiet-6gA4ffVa3EYOhFQ==)
---
|
# I STAND FOR MEDICAL FREEDOM
---
| I did not get the jab. And I fought for your right not to get the jab. The government should not mandate an experimental "vaccine."
---
|
---
|

---
|
---
| Dr. Sheila Furey, the President of VAMFA Virginia Medical Freedom Alliance and I worked very closely together on legislation that I sponsored to push back against onerous and unconstitutional mandates.
---
|

---
|
---
| Pictured: Dr. Robert Malone, Senator Amanda Chase and Dr. Paul Marik.
Dr. Robert Malone was the inventor of the Covid vaccine. Although he took the jab, he later withdrew his support and is now actively exposing the truth about the side effects of the vaccine, to include death.
Dr. Paul Marik spoke as a subject matter expert on several of my medical freedom bills. People want choice not mandates.
---
|
# STANDING FOR THE 2ND AMENDMENT
---
| Many of you heard me speak at the 2020 VCDL Legislative Day at the Capitol when there were well over 50,000 gun loving Virginians who arrived in Virginia.
You can trust me to protect your gun rights. To me it's personal. You can trust me to stand up for the 2nd Amendment.
---

clean_body: | | | | Who has a PROVEN TRACK RECORD? Who has the COURAGE TO STAND UP FOR YOU? | | AMANDA CHASE | Friends, Many of you called me "Trump in Heels." I was honored. Like President Trump I've been fighting the Swamp in Richmond, since 2008 when I first got involved in Virginia politics as a young mother of 4, 3 of those were sons by the way. Many of you have said you are supporting me because I have a backbone and I'm not afraid to stand up for you. I was censured over January 6 and saying the 2020 Presidential Election was stolen. They built a plexiglass wall around my desk for refusing to wear a mask. I learned from my father, who retired as an engineer and my mother who was a school teacher, to ask questions and to think critically. I've served in the Virginia Senate for 8 years from 2016-2023 before retiring to Appomattox, Virginia. Now I live in Senate District 10 and I am seeking the Republican nomination for Virginia Senate in a Special Election this coming December to finish off John McGuire's term in the Virginia Senate, as he goes off to Congress this January. I have both the experience and conservative voting record you can trust to hit the ground running on day one. If you live in Senate District 10 which includes 11 counties, Amelia, Appomattox, Buckingham, Cumberland, Prince Edward, Fluvanna, Goochland, Hanover, the Shady Grove precinct in Henrico, Louisa and Powhatan, you will have the opportunity to vote in a Special Election in a matter of weeks, to fill a state senate seat that John McGuire will vacate as he heads to Congress. This Special Election will most likely take place mid-December in a Republican run off called a Republican mass meeting. Once the exact date and location are decided we will let you know. While there are several candidates vying for the Republican nomination, I'm the only candidate who has actually served in the Virginia Senate as a Senator for 8 years and has an actual conservative voting record you can trust. Any candidate can say they are a conservative or they are pro-2A, but I'm the only candidate running with an actual 8 year voting record you can trust, not just rhetoric. When the going gets tough, will your representative cave to the pressure to "go along, to get along?" Will they be brave enough to ask the tough questions, the questions that you would ask? Will your representative take a stand for you? Will they challenge the status quo, as I did during the assault on our liberties "during Covid," and after the 2020 Presidential Election was stolen? Does the candidate you are supporting have a history of standing up to protect the unborn, medical freedom, the 2nd Amendment, true election integrity including hand counting paper ballots? Talk is cheap. Do they have a history of standing up for what's right? Where were they during the Covid mandates? Did they go along with the vaccine and mask mandates or did they rightly push back? Where were they on January 6? Greetings! I've been with you in the trenches fighting against the gun grab, the Covid mandates, the stolen elections and the fight against medical tyranny, just for starters. People were dying from the jabs. People were getting injured from the jabs. Yet they were being mandated if they wanted to work, continue their education or even access certain venues. It should have been your choice, not a mandate. Where were they? I had to speak up. It was the right thing to do. Our Constitutional rights were being decimated during Covid, and I refused to be silent. Businesses were being crushed as government dictated which businesses were essential and which ones were not essential. So I organized a "Reopen Virginia" rally in Richmond which took place during Governor Northam's press conference. You could actually hear car horns beeping during his press conference. Students were not allowed to return to college unless they were vaxxed. So I introduced legislation to protect students who did not want to get the experimental jab. It seemed I was the only elected official talking about what was important to the people, unconstitutional Covid mandates and the 2020 stolen presidential election. The people knew they could trust me. Voting and participation in our elections is vital to our Constitutional Republic. Yet there are some who want only the party faithful to be able to choose our Republican nominees and allow the masses to have a say in the General Election. Do you remember the Governor's race? You wanted to vote but the process was confusing. Where do you vote? When did you vote? Most had no idea when or where to vote and when they did find out, realized it was too late to sign up. Most voters had no idea there was paperwork to be completed and deadlines in which they had to turn in required GOP created forms. It was the individual county or city GOP chairs that arbitrarily determined deadlines and locations for turning in those forms. Some unit chairs allowed several weeks to turn in the forms, other chairs only gave people a week. Republican voters who wanted to participate in the nomination process for choosing our next Republican nominee for Governor found the voting process confusing. Many missed the arbitrary deadlines that were different for each county and many voters who wanted to participate could not. They had missed the arbitrary deadlines. There were Republican voters who were not members of their local GOP who had a difficult time figuring out how to vote. In a Congressional run off, my elderly parents had to wait hours in 100 degree heat during the height of the Covid mandates. What are we doing? We are discouraging people from participating in the process. When I returned the Senate, I voted to end confusing party processes that disenfranchise and discourage Republicans from voting and choosing our Republican nominees. And I was successful. While some Republicans support this confusing party processes to chose our Republican nominees, I consistently support processes that voters are most familiar with and allow legal recourse for bad actors. This legislation went into effect July 2024 but does not apply to Special Elections, like the one we will have this coming December and January. So please pay attention, so you don't miss the upcoming deadlines and can participate in the process to choose the next Republican nominee to replace John McGuire in the Virginia Senate. If you live in Senate District 10, which includes Amelia, Appomattox, Buckingham, Cumberland, Prince Edward, Fluvanna, Goochland, Henrico, Hanover, Louisa or Powhatan, you will most likely vote in November, December and January. Please be on the look out after this November's General Election for a Special Election to be called to fill an open state senate seat to be vacated by John McGuire who who is expected to win this November. Republicans will chose their nominee in December or January with the General Election expected to be January 7, the day before the General Assembly gavels in. So please stay tuned. There will be an announcement of a Special Election and then 5 days later we are expected to come up with a Republican nominee. Typically a replacement would be selected by only a handful of party faithful who are aware of the election. I started back in July educating you about this Special Election, so that you could be made aware and participate in selecting our Republican nominee. It's important that McGuire's successor is chosen by "We the People". This is no time for inexperience, which is why I threw my hat in the ring to run back in July, as the first candidate to announce. In July, I fully committed to running for this state senate seat and bought a home in conservative Appomattox, where I plan to reside indefinitely. Those who have followed me know I have both the experience and conservative voting record to hit the ground running on day one, when session will already be in progress. While there are many Republicans vying for the seat, I'm the only candidate who will be ready day one when session is already in progress. Talk is cheap. Actions count. While many will say they support issues important to conservatives, yet I'm the only one running with an actual conservative voting record to prove it. While there are a number of candidates seeking the Republican nomination for State Senate in Senate District 10 to finish John McGuire's state senate term, I'm the only candidate who has a proven voting record you can trust 100% of the time. I have a conservative voting record you can trust all 8 years I served in the Virginia Senate: 100% Pro-family, The Family Foundations scorecard, 2016-2023 100% Pro-gun, VCDL Virginia Citizen's Defense League, 2016-2023 A rating, NRA, 2016-2023 100% Pro-small business, National Federation of Independent Business, 2016-2023. You can trust me. I'm experienced. I have a conservative voting record you can trust, and I'm willing to stand when it's important to stand on our conservative principles. Sometimes that means speaking up and challenging the status quo. You have the opportunity to support an award winning effective conservative senator with 8 years of experience as a senator and a 100 percent conservative voting record you can trust and who knows how to win for the people. Today, I ask for your vote if you live in Amelia, Appomattox, Buckingham, Cumberland, Fluvanna, Goochland, Louisa, Prince Edward, Henrico, Hanover and Powhatan in the upcoming Special Election. If you live outside the district, I ask for your most generous donation. You may not be able to vote for me, but I can certainly vote for you. | Virginia Senate, 2016-2023, 8 years of experience as Senator 8 year, conservative voting record The Virginia Chamber of Commerce, A Voting Record, Distinguished Advocate for Virginia Business The American Conservative Union, Conservative Excellence 2016-2023 Legislator of the Year Award, Treasurer's Association of Virginia | KEY LEGISLATIVE WINS | Healthcare Transparency Act Parents & Educators Not Big Business Determine School Start Times Elimination of Mask Mandates in Public Schools Historic Coal Ash Legislation to Reduce Cancer Risks Military Retirement Benefit Exemption Required Notification to Parents of Sexually Explicit Material in Public Schools Committee Meeting Online, Citizens Access to Legislators Online Doesn't Take PAC or Lobbyists Money Voted no on all fees and taxes Voted against all gun control measures 100% Pro-Life from fertilization | LEGISLATIVE AWARDS & ACHIEVEMENTS | 100% VCDL Virginia Citizen's Defense League, 8 years 100% The Family Foundation, 8 years 100% National Federation of Independent Business, 8 years "A" Rating NRA National Rifle Association "Conservative Excellence", The American Conservative Union, 8 years Legislator of the Year, Treasurer's Association of Virginia "A" Rating, Distinguished Advocate for Business, The Virginia Chamber of Commerce Patriot's Award, VAIB Virginia Assembly of Independent Baptists Outstanding Leadership, Sierra Club, In recognition of Leadership on Coal Ash James River Association River Hero, James River Association Industrial Strength Leadership Award, VMA Virginia Manufacturer's Association PAC Veterans For America First, 2023 Virginia Society for Human Life, 2019 | BILLS I'VE INTRODUCED | These are all of the bills that I've sponsored since I was first elected in 2016. It doesn't mean I'm perfect, but through all of the trials, persecution and struggles, it's my faith that I turn to and from which I draw strength during the good times and the more challenging times. I accepted Jesus into my heart when I was in the 2nd grade. As a young teen and young adult my relationship with the Lord became more personal. I felt compelled to go on several missions trips and while in college at Virginia Tech was very involved with Campus Crusade for Christ, which is now called Cru. It was during my college years that I began to study the Bible and my faith strengthened. I remember spending my spring break in Daytona sharing the gospel of Christ with people on the beach. As a young adult who worked full time in management in the finance and banking field, I spent some week nights and weekends visiting people who had visited our church and led several small group Bible studies. As a young mother, I left corporate America to raise and homeschool our 4 children under a religious exemption statue. It was then I began to volunteer for the Family Foundation and led various programs and coops for homeschool families seeking opportunities for their children. It was also then that I began to volunteer for various political campaigns for conservative Republicans. One thing lead to another and my volunteerism lead to serving as campaign staff, political director and even campaign manager for various federal, state and local elected officials. Ultimately, I would run and win a state senate seat, overcoming a 28 year incumbent. I served in the Virginia Senate for 8 years from 2016 to 2023, until I was "defeated" by 375 mail-in and early votes out of 22,000 votes, in which my campaign was denied overseeing of the early voting machines. I contested the race, but was ultimately ignored by the Virginia State Board of Elections and the Republican Party of Virginia. We sought justice legally, but we were ignored. The people of Senate District 12 were disenfranchised but no one had the boldness to investigate our concern, despite our best efforts to fight the injustice. It was a frustrating experience but at the end of the day I trusted the Lord to ultimately bring justice. Many of you have heard me say the Holy Spirit is my GPS. That is true. Like many of you, I too get distracted and take the longer route because I wasn't paying attention. However, my heart is to put God first every morning, listen to His voice and follow the path that He has laid before me through doors that I believe He opens and closes. While I believe my loss was nefarious at best, it was a door God closed. And one I had a hard time accepting because of the appearance of cheating. I've had former employees who had worked for me in the past and been good to literally stab me in the back. They accepted money from other elected officials who I had challenged on various policies to betray and defame my good character and reputation. They came after me politically, professionally and personally. A Richmond General District Court judge granted me a protective order against one employee who continues to follow me to this day. Why? Because I wasn't popular with the other politicians. I wouldn't just go along to get along. I took a stand. Life begins at conception, not 15 weeks, for starters. I refused to approve a Health and Human Resources Cabinet member because they refused to reverse Governor Northam's position regarding healthcare providers ability to prescribe ivermectin and hydroxychloroquine for the treatment of Covid. I refused to rejoin the Republican Senate Caucus until the leader resigned because he had introduced bills to increase taxes and even introduced a gun control bill. I could not in good conscience sit under a leader who was introducing bills only a Democrat would introduce. Standing came at a cost. I had my committee assignments taken away, my seniority taken away and my seat moved to the farthest side of the Virginia Senate chamber and my colleagues . All in an attempt to shame me into submission. No matter what they did to me or said about me, I chose to stand. | TAKING A STAND DURING COVID | REFUSING TO WEAR A MASK | During Covid, I refused to wear a mask. As a result, the Senate staff built a plexiglass wall around my desk. I referred to as my "Wall of Freedom." Overnight, we saw America become a socialist nation. Our basic ability to breathe was now being governed by an overreaching tyrannical government. Even the manufacturer of the masks printed a disclaimer stating the masks did not protect people from the spread of viruses. In fact, it was later proven that masks actually cause harm and increase a person's chances of contracting a respiratory virus. I introduced many bills to make it illegal for employers, educators and even small businesses from violating our civil rights. These decisions of body autonomy we could determine for ourselves. I called on the government to stop restricting our ability to work, get educated or simply perform everyday activities like shop and visit the doctor for simply refusing the experimental jab. I introduced legislation that would allow doctors to prescribe ivermectin and hydroxychloroquine for the treatment of Covid. | | Our Constitutional rights were being violated and I had to take a stand, no matter the cost. Wearing a mask or not should have been a choice, not a mandate. | | My colleagues complied with the mandates. I did not. I was both isolated and ostracized by my colleagues for standing. The Senate Clerk staff put my desk at the back of the room in a far corner. They tried to break me, but I stood strong. | | During the height of Covid, I was running for Governor. During the day I would serve in the Senate of Virginia. In the evenings, I campaigned across the state. No mask. No vaccine. People were desperate for someone who would stand up for them under the Constitutional mandates. | | Here's a picture of the Virginia Senate in 2021, during the height of Covid. We were forced to meet at the Science Museum of Virginia so that we could comply with social distancing mandates, which we've now discovered were a hoax and nothing more than political science. | | During the height of Covid, who stood up for you? Who fought the unconstitutional mandates? I did. And it came at a cost. | | Who can you count on to stand with you in the future? Who has had the courage, fortitude and history of standing with you in the past? Standing wasn't easy. Even comedian Jimmy Kimmel mocked me nationally for refusing to wear a mask. They wanted to break me, but I stood strong in the midst of isolation, being mocked and ridiculed. I continued to challenge the mask and vaccine mandates. They were making decisions based on political science, not science. Today, 4 years later, we know know the truth. You can count on me. I've stood with you in the past. I will stand with you in the future. | | == | I STAND FOR MEDICAL FREEDOM | I did not get the jab. And I fought for your right not to get the jab. The government should not mandate an experimental "vaccine." | | | | Dr. Sheila Furey, the President of VAMFA Virginia Medical Freedom Alliance and I worked very closely together on legislation that I sponsored to push back against onerous and unconstitutional mandates. | | | Pictured: Dr. Robert Malone, Senator Amanda Chase and Dr. Paul Marik. Dr. Robert Malone was the inventor of the Covid vaccine. Although he took the jab, he later withdrew his support and is now actively exposing the truth about the side effects of the vaccine, to include death. Dr. Paul Marik spoke as a subject matter expert on several of my medical freedom bills. People want choice not mandates. | STANDING FOR THE 2ND AMENDMENT | Many of you heard me speak at the 2020 VCDL Legislative Day at the Capitol when there were well over 50,000 gun loving Virginians who arrived in Virginia. You can trust me to protect your gun rights. To me it's personal. You can trust me to stand up for the 2nd Amendment. | | | == | | == Senator Amanda Chase has a 100% rating from VCDL all 8 years she served in the Virginia Senate from 2016-2023. | | | | | | | | | | | Some politicians say that they support the 2nd Amendment. I have both a public history and legislative record of opposing gun control. Action should trump rhetoric. I've actually introduced legislation to repeal the gun control laws that the Democrat controlled General Assembly passed in 2020. | | After I was assaulted on multiple occasions on Capitol grounds, I decided to open carry. It worked. The national protestors that flooded our state capitol finally stopped laying hands on me. I believe with all of my heart that guns save lives. I also believe an armed society is a polite society. | | | Many have taken this picture out of context. My team and I were standing on Bank Street in front of the Virginia Capitol in Richmond with hundreds of pro-2nd Amendment supporters from all walks of life. | | July 4, 2020, I was asked to be speak at a Pro-2nd Amendment Rally in downtown Richmond at our state capitol. I was told it was too dangerous to go. Both Antifa and BLM were supposed to be there. My security team and I made the decision to go anyway. Keep in mind the Richmond Police and just been demoralized by Democrat Mayor Levar Stoney. This picture was taken in the streets of Richmond. | The Democrat Trifecta of 2020 passed scores of gun control legislation like one gun a month, universal background checks, red flag laws and gun free zones could not be created by localities. I spoke out against these bills that were a violation of both our U.S. Constitution and our Virginia Constitution. The Democrats controlled the Virginia Senate, the Virginia House and Governor Ralph Northam was Governor. Legislatively, there was nothing we could do. We didn't have the votes. So I chose to go public and expose to the American people what the Democrats were doing. | Bloomberg and others nefarious actors were dumping millions of dollars into Virginia. It was an all out war on our 2nd Amendment rights. While I knew we didn't have the votes, I chose to be creative and get vocal. | To implement socialism, I knew they would first come for our guns. And they did. Instead of protecting individual rights for people to protect themselves, the Democrats were more interested in protecting the criminals, including violent offenders. | SENATOR CHASE TARGET PRACTICE | == | | STANDING UP FOR INNOCENT LIFE THOSE WHO CAN'T SPEAK UP FOR THEMSELVES | The Pro-family, non-profit group, The Family Foundation issues a legislative scorecard so that Virginians can see how their legislator scores on pro-family issues. For my entire 8 year legislative career, year after year from 2016 through 2023, I received a perfect score of 100%. You can trust that I will stand for life, because I have a voting record you can trust. Not just my words. | | Life begins at fertilization. Even 98% of scientists agree. I introduced a Life Begins at Conception bill. The Senate Rules Committee refused to make a motion to vote up or down on the bill. If they voted for the bill, they would have to agree that life begins at conception. If they voted down the bill, they would oppose the opinion of 98% of scientists. | == Watch as I present SR87 Life Begins at Conception bill on | | I've been honored to speak at Family Foundation organized pro-life rallies at the Virginia State Capitol. After SCOTUS overturned the Landmark Roe vs Wade Case, CNN asked for an interview. I'm not afraid to stand for life. | | | == | I led the effort in the Virginia Senate to defeat the so called Equal Right Amendment, a failed amendment that was resurrected from the 70s. The false claim was that it should be passed to give women equal rights and codify it in the Constitution. The real agenda was to promote gender fluidity, a concept that seemed so foreign until today. I was an outspoken leader against the so called Equal Rights Amendment. Listen as I speak against SJR 284 on the floor of the Virginia Senate. | == | I've worked under two Democrat governors and one Republican governor. There are different strategies that have to be used for each. Under a Democrat governor, we know pro-life legislation will not pass. So do we continue to introduce legislation even when we know it will not pass? I believe that we should and I do. It's important that we put legislators on the record so that voters know how they will vote when we do have the votes and it does count. | | | | | The late Virginia Senator Eva Mae Scott (R-Amelia) and I speak at a press conference organized by The Family Foundation against the so-called Equal Rights Amendment. | STANDING UP FOR SECURE ELECTIONS MAKING SURE YOUR VOTE COUNTS | | When voters in my senate district went to cast their vote in 2020, many said that they couldn't vote because someone had already cast their vote for them. I was determined to investigate and get to the bottom of this, despite efforts to be shamed into not questioning the 2020 Presidential Election results. | | I flew to Arizona to witness the first ever full forensic audit so that I knew what legislation to introduce in Virginia. Clearly there was a national effort to steal our elections and what they were doing in Arizona was happening all across the country, but only Republican controlled legislators were able to investigate. I worked very closely with the Arizona legislature. My hope was to have a full forensic audit right here in Virginia, but the legislature was completely controlled by the Democrats. So I sought to expose what was happening across the country, and eventually right here in Virginia. | | Pictured: Arizona State Senator Wendy Rodgers, Senator Amanda Chase, Representative Mark Finchem and Senator Sonny Borrelli. | | The Voter Confidence Restoration Act was just one of many bills that I introduced to put the guardrails back on our Virginia elections and restore voter confidence in our elections. While my goal was ultimately a full forensic audit of the 2020 election, I knew that wouldn't happen with a Democrat controlled legislature. So, I led the effort to simply go back to our Virginia Election Law best practices prior to covid, back when we required photo ID to vote, we had an election day instead of an election season and there were no drop boxes. | | While in South Dakota at the Mike Lindell Cyber Symposium I founded the National Election Integrity Caucus for legislators across the country. Together we worked on and shared legislative ideas and bills to assist with our election integrity efforts. | | STANDING UP ON JAN 6 | I was there on January 6. But I did not go into the nation's Capitol. Many of us believed the 2020 Presidential Election was stolen and joining the protest at the Capitol in DC with President Trump to exercise our First Amendment right to free speech and to contest the election results. We were counting on Vice President Mike Pence to do the right thing. Unfortunately, he did not and his political career is over. I stand up for true election integrity. As a result, I was censured by the Virginia Senate with 3 Republicans joining. | | | | STANDING UP FOR SAFER COMMUNITIES LOCKING UP VIOLENT CRIMINALS | Over the past 8 years, I have introduced legislation to support our law enforcement to include the Law Enforcement Bill of Rights. I voted against repealing qualified immunity. | | YOU CAN TRUST ME TO STAND | When you are considering who you are going to support for State Senate in District 10, (Appomattox, Amelia, Buckingham, Cumberland, Prince Edward, Fluvanna, Goochland, Louisa, Powhatan, Hanover and the Shady Grove precinct in Henrico) ask yourself who has the experience to start on Day One? The Republican Mass Meeting to fill Senator John McGuire's seat will likely occur this December 2024. The General Election will likely occur on January 7, 2025, the day before session begins. I am the only candidate running for state senate who will be able to hit the ground running on Day 1. In addition, you know that I'm pro-life, pro-gun, pro-school choice, pro-medical freedom, pro-election integrity because I have a history of fighting for you and have a voting record you can trust. I have 8 years of experience as a Virginia Senate. Plus I have an 8 year voting record you can trust. Today, I humbly ask for your financial support and your vote. | | Virginia State Senator (R-District 11) 2016-2023 | | == | | | | | | | | | == | | == | Authorized and Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase | | | | Friends of Amanda Chase | P.O. Box 5811 | Midlothian, VA 23112 US | | | | | |

name: Amanda Chase
subject: Cumberland Sham Debate Cancelled
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase
body:
The Rig is on
| | | | THE CUMBERLAND DEBATE IS CANCELLED
---
---
| Greetings!, Based on recent polling, I'm the front runner in the race for Virginia Senate District 10 to fill John McGuire's term in the Virginia Senate.
While there are a quite a few candidates running, most of you have said you've never heard of the other candidates but you have heard of me.
Why? Because I've been in the trenches for over a decade fighting for your conservative values.
I'm not a newcomer to this fight. I've been a warrior for your conservative values.
Why would I give a platform to candidates who have not been in the fight and haven't done the work? You don't even know who they are.
I'm not participating in sham debates like the one coming up next Friday in Cumberland County, which I never ever agreed to.
I will not be there.
The chair is supporting another candidate and isn't neutral. I'm not going to be strong armed into doing something I feel is rigged.
From the beginning the chair who was installed by one of the campaigns was advised that we would not be participating in his debate, yet continued to promote the event to drive attendance.
He wants to tell you that I dropped out of the debate, which I never agreed to.
This is about trying to take out the front runner in the race for state senate and is a complete set up.
I'm not going to give into bullies who try to strong arm me.
While I love debating and have participated in many debates when I ran for state senate and even governor, I will not participate in sham debates.

---
|
---
When this chairman was asked to retract his statement that falsely said that all campaigns including mine had confirmed they were coming, he said he would send out a retraction, but failed to do so as of this morning. In fact, he doubled down.
The establishment once again is trying to trap me. And I'm not falling for it.
You already know me. You know I represent the conservative values that are important to you.
Please join me in boycotting this debate.
I'll be sending you a list of events across the district shortly so that we can meet face to face.
I won't be participating in sham debates.
---

| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T096d0ad5-3526-44b2-bc7d-984e7020b152/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

|
---
| | | | | Hi Peter, Volunteers are urgently needed in Pennsylvania. With tens of thousands of votes still to be counted, Senator Bob Casey’s race is still too close to call. While votes are still being counted, you can help ensure that Pennsylvanians’ voices are heard. Thousands of already-cast votes have been rejected by their County Board of Elections, but we have the chance to help voters correct their ballots. We’re counting on you to help ensure Pennsylvanians’ voices are heard by contacting these voters and explaining how these mistakes can be corrected – a process called “curing.” [Will you sign up for a shift to help cure ballots in-person or virtually?](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tc8f1b712-0f4b-4ff7-bce2-2815d0820b62/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Voters only have until today, November 12th to correct issues with their ballots – so time is running out:
---
| | [VOLUNTEER TO CURE BALLOTS →](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7889ae4b-9cf9-4acf-bf92-cd570d31dd06/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
|
---
| | | If you are located in or near Pennsylvania, the most effective way to volunteer is to knock on doors. If you are unable to canvass, there are virtual options to call voters from your own home. The Pennsylvania Democrats team will train you before your volunteer shift begins, and will be available to answer any questions you have. Thank you so much for your support at this critical time, Blue Amp Action

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T96b09693-f5dd-4db9-9061-4edca8fd06b2/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Bob MacGuffie
subject: Breaking Commentary:
body:
CT Republicans are now '0 for 51' in Federal Elections -
| | | |
---
| \-- Reporting from the Political Front --
---
---
| | November 12, 2024
Republicans had spectacular electoral success nationally last week. Yet CT Republicans for the last NINE election cycles, have been unable to win either a Congressional or U.S. Senate seat. As the old chestnut about the definition of insanity goes, maybe we should try something different. After 15 years of grassroots efforts I thought I'd share a couple of my experiences, and my thoughts on how to escape this debilitating cycle. My assessment appears below and as an article in today's [Connecticut Centinal](https://lk9rohfbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001KXqIPyqr2Tay5WPQLuhYX3CNd9izN9V09NtQXOclX2JxPlnB9AUi_zQkIBsy_pQG724p6tbFG9e-DsQWzzNu0-BzKBtr4fMxb5jIKmK_Aq-StOyjzk8uQ-azo91xaZ7P87wOXazmdn6KObeYAd1csD22RMsVviJtrczDEXpg9_jtd6GDeqv_HHFBxPFbapEyxmQ5VCp837u42yYM1Fsxh6yyVPDB1_XjblQ6tTLt4BQ=&c=_HAlCAsLWn6gsHLflCTLWT0spNV8zN758Qj-aaUPrVENlhx2tGwjmQ==&ch=23qcp2YOlgl1WBToy_c82aJzI1jLgrVhfkMs4LZlOw4O_pqEK_8GSA==).
CT Republicans Are Now '0 for 51' in Federal Elections \-- Maybe try a new tact founded in REALITY By Bob MacGuffie Now that CT Republicans have endured another statewide defeat at the hands of the merchants of fraud, known as the Democrat Party, maybe we can stop running our races on the political battlefield defined by the American Left. I've been in the political fight publicly for 15 years, since the dawn of the Tea Party in 2009. The issues we then pushed onto the national agenda include limited government, primacy of the individual, unapologetic U.S. sovereignty, constitutional originalism, and free-market economics, among many other well-known derivative issues. The first election cycle in which these conservative-libertarian principles were tested nationally was 2010. They proved a spectacular success in the U.S. House, the chamber closest to the citizenry. Republicans took back the House with an historic 63 seat pick-up, even cowering Obama to admit his party took a "shellacking." And well-deserved it was. At their first electoral opportunity, a large majority of Americans rejected Obama's "fundamental transformation of America," and as we saw Tuesday, an overwhelming majority after experiencing 16 years of it, reject it outright today. However, in 2010 the party told us that it was "different" in Connecticut, and with a couple exceptions, Republican candidates then largely rejected our newly energized grassroots agenda in races for the state assembly and senate. Republicans picked up one state senate seat and a handful of assembly seats in 2010, but remained in a deep minority status - about 35% Republican in each chamber. But this weak showing still seemed to give Larry Cafero, Assembly minority leader and John McKinney, Senate minority leader some kind of encouragement. Their strategy was to stress that they were "fiscally conservative" or "fiscally responsible" and stay away from conservative social issues. Whenever pressed by the state's ultra-liberal media for their positions on social issues, they performed a verbal gymnastics act which basically aped the Democrat positioning on the issues. Larry was known to also say, "I don't do the three Gs (guns, God and gold). I pleaded with them to run on the full Tea Party agenda, which their party's base was now championing in local events throughout the state. They told me that if the candidates wholly embraced that grassroots agenda, they would lose seats in both Houses. I would acknowledge they might well lose some seats initially, largely because they had been building a transactional constituency, predisposed to move on to the next candidate whistling a sweeter tune to their susceptible ears. My contention was that if they campaigned on the full breadth of Republican, indeed foundational conservative principles re-established a century ago, they would attract a truly dedicated and loyal base which would not be coaxed away by future transient campaign messaging. But Larry and John would have none of it. They and other elected Republicans wouldn't even begin referencing the Democrats as 'Socialist Democrats', or at least refer to their worst policies as 'Socialist'. Such branding would have proven to be a truth-ringing handicap for the Democrats over the ensuing decade. Indeed, with their strategy of shaving the edges of the Democrat positions, they did pick up seats in the next two elections, drawing even in the Senate, and within seven seats in the Assembly. At that point the Tea Party issues had merged with Trump's rising Populist agenda into a Conservative-MAGA agenda which prevailed nationally in 2016. However, post Trump's inauguration, Republicans of all stripes faced a ferocious and relentless, all-quarters attack from the Democrats, animated by the organized American Left, which had been caught flat-footed by the highly effective Trump-MAGA movement. Withering attacks came daily as the Left, aided by corporate media, endeavored to make Trump and incumbent Republicans radioactive. They were successful - Pelosi was returned as Speaker of the House. In Connecticut, Republicans lost a third (6) of their senate seats in the '18 mid-term cycle - both CT chambers were now back where they were in 2010. When the going got tough, those Obama-era seat pick-ups were utterly unreliable. We in the base were not surprised, because those new voters were transient, and not dedicated to any specific principles. Republicans calling themselves "Moderates" managed to give the Party a most tenuous hold on power, only to see it vaporize under practiced Leftist attack. After all, nobody can pose as a "Moderate" like a Democrat can. Skirmishing around a Democrat-defined middle ground is a LOSER for Republicans. I covered the failed dynamics in an article, [The Race vs. Jim Himes Cannot be Contested on a Democrat-Defined Political Battlefield](https://lk9rohfbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001KXqIPyqr2Tay5WPQLuhYX3CNd9izN9V09NtQXOclX2JxPlnB9AUi_7Lxsv8EDUIdR5PAkkWLqQ0BhNq5zgIt2C8AOKS1LUtBptlvRkSCB-Cb0T0b_gGnt1Y52d_2G0P3I_h0t9Z8U0YelLLQ4MZJRvTotluWLyueap7OQL0cJj8q8GLwV62PT5FZPxAhZcVSgWRjYE792rFUxOLs8jO3r311-jzf0nHe4wgmhrPRP0t0gGCA3FBJcGkmofxnARUDA0kryqxHXTnoiRLlv0lh3w==&c=_HAlCAsLWn6gsHLflCTLWT0spNV8zN758Qj-aaUPrVENlhx2tGwjmQ==&ch=23qcp2YOlgl1WBToy_c82aJzI1jLgrVhfkMs4LZlOw4O_pqEK_8GSA==). The essence is that the Left makes both our most effective public figures and our most potent issues "radioactive," thereby smearing them and discouraging Republican candidates from associating with either the people or the issues. If we comply, this gives the Democrats a significant playing-field advantage. We must NOT play along. Our stance is right - and we must stand on our foundational, conservative principles. This was demonstrated yet again a couple weeks back in the Fourth District congressional debate between Jim Himes and Mike Goldstein. Himes got his way with his false-premises, which were accepted by the moderator and Mr. Goldstein. Sure, there was some skirmishing around the issues. But the underlying leftist, collectivist principles, which Mr. Himes regularly conceals with his well-vocabularied word salad, were never challenged. Nor were his cheap ploys, gambits, and strawman arguments ever specifically exposed to his constituents in the room and online. The Republicans' perennial mistake was again taking Democrats at face value - a patina which is a calculated construct designed to prevail in Fairfield County's version of "politics-as-usual." Indeed, in Himes' post-election e-mail he said, "I also want to thank both my opponents. I believe we engaged in a respectful and productive campaign. If only Washington, DC operated a tad more like Fairfield County." Translation: I appreciate that you performed in your usual self-defeating manner that CT Democrats have come to depend on - and I deeply appreciate that you offered no fight. This syndrome will continue to prevail until the politics aren't 'usual' anymore. So, in Connecticut we're now 0 for 51 in congressional and U.S. senate races since Chris Shays' last slim victory in 2006. To shatter this dynamic, it must be recognized that we're opposed not only by Democrats, but by a politico-media combine including the activist Left and legacy corporate media - Hearst and other corporates in print, and Comcast, Cox, the networks and others in the electronic channels. Over the past 20 years, the combination of these gaslighting forces have severely traumatized a majority of the Connecticut voting citizenry into believing profound falsehoods about American culture and the politics which flow from it. I covered the implications of Hearst buying 13 of the leading town papers in the 17 towns of Connecticut's 4th District in my article, [Free Speech is Suffocating Under Blue-Captured CT's 'Iron Cloak' of Corporate Media](https://lk9rohfbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001KXqIPyqr2Tay5WPQLuhYX3CNd9izN9V09NtQXOclX2JxPlnB9AUi_w2kWHlrIfl6LhS5v61ZJq9FEyjwRBhGtPWgP1khr59SUSHhImWiQJZFSVJXbeWKH3lEXgTTQ9wV0BnQz1IJVXIY_PXof259ZEsJ7eU_4pf5SgvYjuQbrVpeEO9eYG_vJOJ3MoMRyaNFisdV1f0CHacCUQWGWdW4pufQfY7zGIBp4UyCU8CeirWhKn5D5N-TPnulMUfHvV5hQhf1MgERWSIbsQXQZ_TJ8Yw9EbUcHbSK&c=_HAlCAsLWn6gsHLflCTLWT0spNV8zN758Qj-aaUPrVENlhx2tGwjmQ==&ch=23qcp2YOlgl1WBToy_c82aJzI1jLgrVhfkMs4LZlOw4O_pqEK_8GSA==). The media's protective cover provides an incalculable assist to CT's Democrat federal delegation. Tuesday's national results indicate that a majority of Americans have indeed awakened, but leading our traumatized CT neighbors from this debilitating media-induced trance is a daunting challenge. We can start by facing reality with both the electorate and Republican leadership. When you speak with Connecticut's 'Unaffiliateds' (some 40% of the electorate), they will tell you they are unaffiliated because they believe the Democrats are deceptive and on the wrong track - and that Republicans really don't seem to stand for anything. We've been told this endless times, both on the campaign trail and in private settings. Shaving the edges around Democrat positions is a proven loser. As one staunch CT conservative operative is fond of saying, "how 'bout we try losing my way for a change!" Those of us with knowledge of recent history know that today's Democrat agenda is driven along the lines of several Cultural Marxist-derivative revolutions which played out in the 20th century - the censorship, the polarization, the demonization, the struggle sessions, the lawfare. The attacks focus on religion because it is an authority higher than government, on family because it's a loyalty higher than government, and on private property because it represents our individual liberty and freedom to act. This is the reality we face. If you have doubts ask anyone you know of Eastern European descent over age 60 who grew up behind the Iron Curtain. To a one they will tell you they escaped that oppression in their youth, horrified only to have it blossom into their lives in America today. A majority of Americans know this to be our reality. The Conservative-MAGA-Republican agenda is pointing us in the direction of retaining and reestablishing our freedoms. It certainly applies in Connecticut as well as elsewhere. After two decades of having our clocks cleaned by a deceptive Democrat machine, Connecticut Republican leadership needs to face reality, stop playing footsie with Democrats, call out their entire fraudulent combine for the gaslighting subversive operation it is - and stand our ground... emphasis on the last phrase. The CT media needs to be confronted, rather than tip-toeing around them. If we're to be smeared by media, it ought to be for taking a staunchly-held principled stance in reality, rather than for inauthentically aping our opponents' positions. President Trump's recent campaign just shattered the legacy media - we can capitalize on that feat right here in Connecticut. Demographic analysis of Trump's support shows identity politics is a loser - we should not genuflect to it in any way, shape or form. Here's a stance drawing a moral line: No minor under the age of 21 should be able to mutilate or drug their body for the purposes of transitioning from one sex to another - period. I believe it to be a state issue, not federal, but it is a clear stance against the insanity promulgated by the Democrats. Gender dysphoria is a mental problem. And get the sexualization of children out of the grade schools. Make our stand on behalf of parents' innate rights to raise their children according to their moral code, not that of school administrators and the Marxist agenda-setters behind them. Constantly force the Democrats to defend the insanity of their policies. Here's a couple Republican state senators taking a stance most recently. Len Fasano was quoted extensively in this recent [Article](https://lk9rohfbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001KXqIPyqr2Tay5WPQLuhYX3CNd9izN9V09NtQXOclX2JxPlnB9AUi_zQkIBsy_pQGfbbZcSpmwKq9iIA5EAsmvDGN7qgW9ygaiomNnsutkZRnkx9H1jwKZl2sdWBjXWBupydJRh5zm-B5OwfaWZljWSTV7G5jHaEDcQ6-4HJf4OnzJ6lcQ2wjubVNkXaucod3gUNUPxWi288Tsy9wRZeoClUa_2oSzAz8sElOgejoUZ_Kl7t4ZM40dvojt_NAILxFunleOh4kdMICNAkiILq2qg==&c=_HAlCAsLWn6gsHLflCTLWT0spNV8zN758Qj-aaUPrVENlhx2tGwjmQ==&ch=23qcp2YOlgl1WBToy_c82aJzI1jLgrVhfkMs4LZlOw4O_pqEK_8GSA==). Among other successful techniques deployed in the cycle, Fasano said, "don't be afraid to take on the press." Also - when the press tried their usual intimidation routine on him, Senator Rob Sampson wasn't impressed. He countered: "Retract my statement? Hell no. Connecticut Democrats should be ashamed of themselves for accusing me of undermining election officials... Connecticut Democrats continue to undermine our American values and culture, making our state less affordable and less safe, and undermining the sanctity of our election process." Senator Sampson is right - and we all know it. The public should be reminded of it constantly. Senator Sampson is showing us the way. As Margaret Thatcher said, "First you win the argument and then you win the vote." That approach enabled the Iron Lady to both capture the leadership of her party, and ultimately transform a slovenly economy and electorate into a truly reinvigorated society, ready to co-lead the western powers at an historical moment. So, let's get on with it. Let's organize and energize Republicans - and the current growing ranks of young Conservative-MAGA CT voters who are ready to save their generation the Socialist Democrats. The time is late in Connecticut, but let's get on the march and join the rest of an awakening nation.
---

| In Liberty,
Bob MacGuffie bobmac@rightprinciples.com

Yet CT Republicans for the last NINE election cycles, have been unable to win either a Congressional or U.S. Senate seat. As the old chestnut about the definition of insanity goes, maybe we should try something different. After 15 years of grassroots efforts I thought I'd share a couple of my experiences, and my thoughts on how to escape this debilitating cycle. My assessment appears below and as an article in today's ==. CT Republicans Are Now '0 for 51' in Federal Elections \-- Maybe try a new tact founded in REALITY By Bob MacGuffie Now that CT Republicans have endured another statewide defeat at the hands of the merchants of fraud, known as the Democrat Party, maybe we can stop running our races on the political battlefield defined by the American Left. I've been in the political fight publicly for 15 years, since the dawn of the Tea Party in 2009. The issues we then pushed onto the national agenda include limited government, primacy of the individual, unapologetic U.S. sovereignty, constitutional originalism, and free-market economics, among many other well-known derivative issues. The first election cycle in which these conservative-libertarian principles were tested nationally was 2010. They proved a spectacular success in the U.S. House, the chamber closest to the citizenry. Republicans took back the House with an historic 63 seat pick-up, even cowering Obama to admit his party took a "shellacking." And well-deserved it was. At their first electoral opportunity, a large majority of Americans rejected Obama's "fundamental transformation of America," and as we saw Tuesday, an overwhelming majority after experiencing 16 years of it, reject it outright today. However, in 2010 the party told us that it was "different" in Connecticut, and with a couple exceptions, Republican candidates then largely rejected our newly energized grassroots agenda in races for the state assembly and senate. Republicans picked up one state senate seat and a handful of assembly seats in 2010, but remained in a deep minority status - about 35% Republican in each chamber. But this weak showing still seemed to give Larry Cafero, Assembly minority leader and John McKinney, Senate minority leader some kind of encouragement. Their strategy was to stress that they were "fiscally conservative" or "fiscally responsible" and stay away from conservative social issues. Whenever pressed by the state's ultra-liberal media for their positions on social issues, they performed a verbal gymnastics act which basically aped the Democrat positioning on the issues. Larry was known to also say, "I don't do the three Gs (guns, God and gold). I pleaded with them to run on the full Tea Party agenda, which their party's base was now championing in local events throughout the state. They told me that if the candidates wholly embraced that grassroots agenda, they would lose seats in both Houses. I would acknowledge they might well lose some seats initially, largely because they had been building a transactional constituency, predisposed to move on to the next candidate whistling a sweeter tune to their susceptible ears. My contention was that if they campaigned on the full breadth of Republican, indeed foundational conservative principles re-established a century ago, they would attract a truly dedicated and loyal base which would not be coaxed away by future transient campaign messaging. But Larry and John would have none of it. Such branding would have proven to be a truth-ringing handicap for the Democrats over the ensuing decade. Withering attacks came daily as the Left, aided by corporate media, endeavored to make Trump and incumbent Republicans radioactive. They were successful - Pelosi was returned as Speaker of the House. A migrant caravan traveling from southern Mexico to the United States has shrunk to about half its original size following Donald Trump’s victory last week.
